Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single t-shirt model or a promotional kit available for sale within the mini e-commerce.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the product, useful for clean page paths.

name.description: The primary name of the product (e.g., 'Camiseta Comunicação Preta', 'Kit Promocional').

shortDescription.description: A concise description of the product, suitable for display on product cards or listings.

fullDescription.description: A comprehensive description of the product, detailing features, materials, and context, displayed on its individual page.

price.description: The current selling price of the product. Stored as a number for potential calculations; display formatting (e.g., 'R$') should be handled by the UI.

imageUrls.description: A list of URLs or paths to high-resolution images of the product, used for galleries and main product visuals.

availableSizes.description: A list of all sizes in which the product is available (e.g., 'PP', 'P', 'M', 'G', 'GG', 'XGG').

color.description: The specific color variant of the t-shirt, if applicable (e.g., 'preta', 'off-white'). This field may be null or absent for kits or multi-color products.

isKit.description: A flag indicating whether this product represents a promotional kit or bundle (true) or a single item (false).

oldPrice.description: The original price of the product, displayed to show a discount when the 'price' is lower. Stored as a number.

active.description: A boolean flag indicating if the product is currently available for purchase and visible on the site.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's reservation or purchase request, initiated on the website and finalized via WhatsApp.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

customerName.description: The full name of the customer who placed this order.

customerWhatsapp.description: The WhatsApp contact number provided by the customer for order finalization.

productName.description: The name of the product or kit selected by the customer in the order form.

selectedSize.description: The chosen size for the product(s) in this order.

quantity.description: The total number of units of the selected product(s) in this order.

paymentMethod.description: The payment method chosen by the customer (e.g., 'Pix', 'Parcelado').

notes.description: Any additional comments or specific instructions provided by the customer for this order.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the order was initially created and saved.

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'Pending WhatsApp Contact', 'Contacted', 'Confirmed', 'Cancelled').

definition.description: Stores details for each t-shirt model or promotional kit. Documents in this collection are publicly readable by all users to display the product catalog. Includes fields like 'color', 'isKit', and 'oldPrice' for comprehensive product representation, beyond the core fields mentioned in 'firebase.firestore.collections.produtos.fields'.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product or kit.

definition.description: Records each customer's reservation or purchase request. Documents are created by unauthenticated users via the order form and include all necessary order details, timestamps, and status. Designed for Authorization Independence as each order document is self-contained.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer order or reservation.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed with two primary top-level collections: `/products` and `/pedidos_iap_camisetas`. This approach ensures clear structural segregation and adheres to all core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and supporting Query Ability Patterns (QAPs).

1.  **`/products/{productId}`:** This collection stores the definitions of all available t-shirts and kits. Its security posture is entirely public, meaning any user can read all product documents. This simplicity is crucial for `Authorization Independence` because no complex access checks or `get()` calls to other documents are required. The products are self-contained and uniformly accessible. For `QAPs`, allowing public read on `/products` means `list` queries will return all available products, as intended, without the server needing to filter results based on user-specific permissions. This is a secure and efficient pattern for public catalogs.

2.  **`/pedidos_iap_camisetas/{orderId}`:** This collection stores each customer's order or reservation. It is designed for `Authorization Independence` by making each `Order` document entirely self-sufficient. An order is created by an unauthenticated user, and the document itself contains all necessary information (`customerName`, `productName`, `quantity`, `paymentMethod`, `createdAt`, `status`). There is no reliance on parent documents or external entities for its inherent access control for the client submitting the order. To manage these orders, an administrator would need to authenticate and have their `uid` validated against a separate, highly restricted `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection (not directly part of this user-facing flow but essential for backend management). For `QAPs`, the rules would permit `create` operations by unauthenticated users (`request.auth.uid == null`), but would explicitly deny `list` or `read` operations for such users. This ensures that no anonymous client can 'filter' or 'discover' existing orders. Only authenticated administrators, whose roles are checked externally, would be granted `read` access to the entire collection, fulfilling QAPs for administrative oversight without exposing sensitive data.
